title: Add `--format` to `podman image scp`
body:
Add `--format` (oci-archive, docker-archive) to pass through to `podman save`. Default is unchanged (no `--format`) so `podman save` uses its own default. Document that `scp` is not storage-to-storage and only archive formats are supported.

Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28183
Fixes: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-4403

#### Checklist

Ensure you have completed the following checklist for your pull request to be reviewed:
<!-- Use [x] to mark as done, or click the checkbox after opening PR -->

the sign-off email address. See [CONTRIBUTING.md](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs)
for more information.
- [x] Referenced issues using `Fixes: #00000` in commit message (if applicable)
- [x] [Tests](https://github.com/containers/podman/tree/main/test#readme) have been added/updated (or no tests are needed)
- [x] [Documentation](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/README.md) has been updated (or no documentation changes are needed)
- [x] All commits pass `make validatepr` (format/lint checks)
- [x] [Release note](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/guide/release-notes.md) entered in the section below (or `None` if no user-facing changes)

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
Write `None` if there are no user-facing changes, otherwise enter your release note below.
Include "action required" if users need to take action when upgrading.
-->

```release-note
The `podman image scp` now supports `--format` (`oci-archive` and `docker-archive`) by forwarding it to `podman save`.
```

comments.body: Ideally I think the command should do the right thing automatically (and if that implied adding support for `dir:`, well, do that), but I’ll leave the decision to Podman maintainers.
One nit on manpages. Code LGTM.
@mtrmac Would tarring up the directory and sending it be sufficient? Seems like a good follow-on PR.

I think tarring up a `dir` and sending it over is the closest to “accurate” we can now get. (I don’t know whether we need to worry about the client and server getting out of sync.)

And if we, hypothetically, added that `dir:` code, who would want to use `--format` afterwards?

> I think tarring up a dir and sending it over is the closest to “accurate” we can now get. (I don’t know whether we need to worry about the client and server getting out of sync.)

I mean on a local copy not but given this copies over ssh I would say that is very likely to be a problem to get version mismatch between hosts so if that format is not supposed to be stable across versions that will be a problem once we break it?
Seems like a good addition as an option to `--format`, then, if you are confident you have matching versions.
I have addressed the documentation issues. Should I create a feature request for a `dir` format for image loading via a remote client? @Luap99 @mheon @mtrmac
@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL for final review and merging.
I've restarted the tests, an awful lot of red at the moment here.

LGTM
One documentation question that can be tended to later if necessary

The four Fedora tests are flaking just about everywhere this morn.  I'll leave the final merge to @mheon or @Luap99
